title: Why Does the Moon?
poem_text:
Why does the moon wear silver shoes?
And where do all the firefly blues?
How do the stars know how to shine?
And why’s the sky not made of pine?

Can clouds get tired of floating high?
Do raindrops ever wonder why?
If shadows had a secret name,
Would they play hide-and-seek the same?

Does the wind keep a diary too?
And what’s the color of a shoe
When no one’s looking—tell me true?
What do *you* think the dark might do?
cover_context: A child lying in bed, gazing out the window at a glowing moon and fireflies, while shadows dance on the walls.
style_prompt: Children's musical poem, acoustic guitar and lo-fi beat, 105 BPM, dreamy and wondering, spacious, leaves room to think, clear warm vocal, every word easy to understand
